english_name: The Second Epistle of Paul the Apostle to Timothy

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc autem scito, quod in novissimis diebus instabunt tempora periculosa:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο δὲ γίνωσκε ὅτι ἐν ἐσχάταις ἡμέραις ἐνστήσονται καιροὶ χαλεποί
verses.king_james: This know also, that in the last days perilous times shall come.

verses.latin_vulgate: erunt homines seipsos amantes, cupidi, elati, superbi, blasphemi, parentibus non obedientes, ingrati, scelesti,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔσονται γὰρ οἱ ἄνθρωποι φίλαυτοι φιλάργυροι ἀλαζόνες ὑπερήφανοι βλάσφημοι γονεῦσιν ἀπειθεῖς ἀχάριστοι ἀνόσιοι
verses.king_james: For men shall be lovers of their own selves, covetous, boasters, proud, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,

verses.latin_vulgate: sine affectione, sine pace, criminatores, incontinentes, immites, sine benignitate,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄστοργοι ἄσπονδοι διάβολοι ἀκρατεῖς ἀνήμεροι ἀφιλάγαθοι
verses.king_james: Without natural affection, trucebreakers, false accusers, incontinent, fierce, despisers of those that are good,

verses.latin_vulgate: proditores, protervi, tumidi, et voluptatum amatores magis quam Dei:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προδόται προπετεῖς τετυφωμένοι φιλήδονοι μᾶλλον ἢ φιλόθεοι
verses.king_james: Traitors, heady, highminded, lovers of pleasures more than lovers of God;

verses.latin_vulgate: habentes speciem quidem pietatis, virtutem autem ejus abnegantes. Et hos devita:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔχοντες μόρφωσιν εὐσεβείας τὴν δὲ δύναμιν αὐτῆς ἠρνημένοι καὶ τούτους ἀποτρέπου
verses.king_james: Having a form of godliness, but denying the power thereof: from such turn away.

verses.latin_vulgate: ex his enim sunt qui penetrant domos, et captivas ducunt mulierculas oneratas peccatis, quæ ducuntur variis desideriis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκ τούτων γάρ εἰσιν οἱ ἐνδύνοντες εἰς τὰς οἰκίας καὶ αἰχμαλωτεύοντες τὰ γυναικάρια σεσωρευμένα ἁμαρτίαις ἀγόμενα ἐπιθυμίαις ποικίλαις
verses.king_james: For of this sort are they which creep into houses, and lead captive silly women laden with sins, led away with divers lusts,

verses.latin_vulgate: semper discentes, et numquam ad scientiam veritatis pervenientes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντοτε μανθάνοντα καὶ μηδέποτε εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν ἀληθείας ἐλθεῖν δυνάμενα
verses.king_james: Ever learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quemadmodum autem Jannes et Mambres restiterunt Moysi: ita et hi resistunt veritati, homines corrupti mente, reprobi circa fidem;
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃν τρόπον δὲ ἰάννης καὶ ἰαμβρῆς ἀντέστησαν μωϋσεῖ οὕτως καὶ οὗτοι ἀνθίστανται τῇ ἀληθείᾳ ἄνθρωποι κατεφθαρμένοι τὸν νοῦν ἀδόκιμοι περὶ τὴν πίστιν
verses.king_james: Now as Jannes and Jambres withstood Moses, so do these also resist the truth: men of corrupt minds, reprobate concerning the faith.

verses.latin_vulgate: sed ultra non proficient: insipientia enim eorum manifesta erit omnibus, sicut et illorum fuit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ οὐ προκόψουσιν ἐπὶ πλεῖον ἡ γὰρ ἄνοια αὐτῶν ἔκδηλος ἔσται πᾶσιν ὡς καὶ ἡ ἐκείνων ἐγένετο
verses.king_james: But they shall proceed no further: for their folly shall be manifest unto all *men*, as theirs also was.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu autem assecutus es meam doctrinam, institutionem, propositum, fidem, longanimitatem, dilectionem, patientiam,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σὺ δὲ παρηκολούθηκάς μου τῇ διδασκαλίᾳ τῇ ἀγωγῇ τῇ προθέσει τῇ πίστει τῇ μακροθυμίᾳ τῇ ἀγάπῃ τῇ ὑπομονῇ
verses.king_james: But thou hast fully known my doctrine, manner of life, purpose, faith, longsuffering, charity, patience,

verses.latin_vulgate: persecutiones, passiones: qualia mihi facta sunt Antiochiæ, Iconii, et Lystris: quales persecutiones sustinui, et ex omnibus eripuit me Dominus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῖς διωγμοῖς τοῖς παθήμασιν οἷά μοι ἐγένετο ἐν ἀντιοχείᾳ ἐν ἰκονίῳ ἐν λύστροις οἵους διωγμοὺς ὑπήνεγκα καὶ ἐκ πάντων με ἐῤῥύσατο ὁ κύριος
verses.king_james: Persecutions, afflictions, which came unto me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra; what persecutions I endured: but out of *them* all the Lord delivered me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et omnes, qui pie volunt vivere in Christo Jesu, persecutionem patientur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πάντες δὲ οἱ θέλοντες εὐσεβῶς ζῆν ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ διωχθήσονται
verses.king_james: Yea, and all that will live godly in Christ Jesus shall suffer persecution.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mali autem homines et seductores proficient in pejus, errantes, et in errorem mittentes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πονηροὶ δὲ ἄνθρωποι καὶ γόητες προκόψουσιν ἐπὶ τὸ χεῖρον πλανῶντες καὶ πλανώμενοι
verses.king_james: But evil men and seducers shall wax worse and worse, deceiving, and being deceived.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu vero permane in iis quæ didicisti, et credita sunt tibi: sciens a quo didiceris:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σὺ δὲ μένε ἐν οἷς ἔμαθες καὶ ἐπιστώθης εἰδὼς παρὰ τίνος ἔμαθες
verses.king_james: But continue thou in the things which thou hast learned and hast been assured of, knowing of whom thou hast learned *them*;

verses.latin_vulgate: et quia ab infantia sacras litteras nosti, quæ te possunt instruere ad salutem, per fidem quæ est in Christo Jesu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅτι ἀπὸ βρέφους τὰ ἱερὰ γράμματα οἶδας τὰ δυνάμενά σε σοφίσαι εἰς σωτηρίαν διὰ πίστεως τῆς ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: And that from a child thou hast known the holy scriptures, which are able to make thee wise unto salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis Scriptura divinitus inspirata utilis est ad docendum, ad arguendum, ad corripiendum, et erudiendum in justitia:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶσα γραφὴ θεόπνευστος καὶ ὠφέλιμος πρὸς διδασκαλίαν πρὸς ἔλεγχον πρὸς ἐπανόρθωσιν πρὸς παιδείαν τὴν ἐν δικαιοσύνῃ
verses.king_james: All scripture *is* given by inspiration of God, and *is* profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut perfectus sit homo Dei, ad omne opus bonum instructus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα ἄρτιος ᾖ ὁ τοῦ θεοῦ ἄνθρωπος πρὸς πᾶν ἔργον ἀγαθὸν ἐξηρτισμένος
verses.king_james: That the man of God may be perfect, throughly furnished unto all good works.
